scope: Qwen2.5 BPE tokenizer pure-hexa port for ALM r12 trainer
phase_pending: PHASE-2 (vocab+merges parse), PHASE-3 (encode loop), PHASE-4 (vs-reference smoke)

files.smoke_check: PASS — inline-tiny-JSON range scan + b2u 256-entry table

what_landed.QwenTokenizer_struct: 8-field struct (loaded, vocab_size, vocab_keys, vocab_ids, merges_a, merges_b, b2u, u2b)
what_landed.build_b2u: GPT-2 byte-to-unicode escape table — 33..126 + 161..172 + 174..255 identity, remaining 68 bytes mapped to U+0100+k. Smoke verified len=256.
what_landed.find_substring_index: Linear naive scan O(nm); good enough for single-shot vocab/merges key lookup.
what_landed.find_matching_close: Tracks JSON {} or [] depth + string-literal escape sequences. Smoke verified on tiny JSON: vocab range 34..52, merges range 63..75.
what_landed.load_qwen_tokenizer: Phase-1 skeleton: locates vocab + merges substrings in the raw 7 MB JSON. Inner key/id/merge parse PENDING; returns loaded=0 to signal 'not ready' so trainer falls back to text_to_ids_stub.
what_landed.encode_bytes_bpe: API surface only; returns [] when loaded=0.
what_landed.smoke_inline_json: Tiny synthetic JSON {model:{vocab:{a:0,b:1,c:2},merges:[a b,b c]}} — proves range scan + b2u correctness without 7 MB load.

what_NOT_landed.vocab_inner_parse: Walking the vocab object range and emitting (key,id) pairs. With 152064 entries and pure-hexa interpreted execution + O(n) string append per push, naive implementation will take minutes. Real fix: small C FFI hashmap (insert/lookup/iter) so the hot path stays in compiled code. Skeleton currently emits empty vocab arrays.
what_NOT_landed.merges_inner_parse: ~50K merge rules; same O(n²) interpreter cost concerns. Same FFI fix.
what_NOT_landed.encode_loop: Greedy lowest-rank-merge loop — algorithmic core. Depends on vocab + merges populated.
what_NOT_landed.byte_to_unicode_escape_in_encode: Plumbed via b2u table but not yet connected to encode pipeline.
what_NOT_landed.vs_reference_smoke: Compare encode('안녕') and encode('hello world') ids vs Qwen tokenizer ground truth. Need to capture reference output once (pure-hexa cannot import HF tokenizers crate); save to JSON for byte-exact comparison.

honest_eta_for_remaining_phases.phase_2_vocab_merges_parser_with_FFI_has: 8-12h focused work (4h FFI hashmap C lib + 4h vocab/merges walker + 2-4h iteration)
honest_eta_for_remaining_phases.phase_3_encode_loop: 4-8h focused work (1h byte-to-unicode + 2h pre-tokenize regex equivalent + 4h merge loop + 1h vocab lookup)
honest_eta_for_remaining_phases.phase_4_vs_reference_smoke: 2-4h (capture reference, write byte-exact comparator, debug discrepancies)

interpreter_perf_note.observation: Reading 7 MB tokenizer.json + scanning bytes via stage0 interpreter timed out >2 min during smoke attempt. Single read_file(7 MB) into hexa string + per-byte byte_at() (which currently does substring + char_code) = O(n) substring allocation per byte ⇒ O(n²) = 49 trillion ops nominally; pathological.
fix_options: (a) read_file_bytes returns array<int>; access via direct array indexing — O(1) per byte. Verify hexa intrinsic exists or wire via FFI to mmap.
(b) Compile tokenizer_qwen.hexa to C via hexa_cc and run native — bypass interpreter entirely.
(c) Write the parser in pure C as a tiny hxtok library, expose hexa FFI surface (load/encode/free).
interpreter_perf_note.recommended: (c) — 200-400 LOC C, links cleanly with existing hxblas/hxnccl/hxqwen14b ecosystem, sidesteps both interpreter perf and hexa string indexing quirks.

blockers.for_trainer_wiring: Tokenizer must exist before train_loop can produce real Qwen ids; current trainer uses text_to_ids_stub (vocab=16) which makes loss=log(16)≈2.77 invariant.
blockers.for_full_r12_launch: Forward+backward+tokenizer ALL three must be real. Backward is the higher-priority blocker (more LOC, more domain expertise) but tokenizer is a hard prereq for measuring meaningful loss curve.

policy_compliance.honest_reporting: OK — Phase-1 only landed, Phase-2/3/4 explicitly marked NOT_LANDED with concrete ETA
policy_compliance.feedback_one_shot_best: OK — did not attempt full 7 MB parse on stage0 after seeing perf wall; documented FFI hashmap recommendation
